changedFiles.reason: Defines the critical-review scope, official sources, evidence-model acceptance criteria, and fail-closed verification requirements.
Records the review findings, design corrections, guideline compliance, and verification evidence.

Separates candidate construction from freshness, defines a normative evidence record and result state machine, binds supported adapters to authoritative platform candidates, and conditions merge-safe conclusions on no-stale-bypass policy.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Use official platform documentation for behavioral and tier claims.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: GitHub Merge Queue and merge_group behavior, plus GitLab pipelines, variables, Merge Train bypass, and enforcement claims were checked against linked official documentation; the final independent accuracy review reported no findings.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Keep current implementation facts distinct from future design requirements.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: The Status section and evidence-contract text explicitly distinguish the existing ai-pre-merge command from the future adapter evidence artifact and runtime.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Keep AI Cockpit Core platform-neutral and derive conclusions from explicit evidence rather than platform labels.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: The v1 evidence object, ordered result rules, and conclusion matrix use construction, freshness, policy, Core, and evidence states independent of platform names.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Use canonical glossary terms and preserve the existing Work Item Recommendation semantics.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: The design explicitly keeps ready_for_review independent from candidate mergeSafety and uses the glossary's Work Item governance meaning.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Keep unsupported editions outside the detailed compatibility protocol.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: GitLab Free remains a concise head-only non-correspondence statement with no synthetic coordinator, credential, or protected-push protocol.

risk.detail: The document is implementation-driving and a mistaken state transition could authorize stale candidates; three iterative five-strategy reviews and official fact checks resolved all actionable findings.

observedIssues: The prior candidate-mode taxonomy incorrectly made target-integrated construction imply freshness and could not represent target-integrated diagnostic candidates.
The prior design overloaded Cockpit ready_for_review with merge-safety semantics.
GitLab Merge Train presence was treated as sufficient despite documented bypass and skip_merge_train-without-restart behavior.
The prior Core contract lacked a versioned adapter evidence record, authoritative candidate/base mapping, invalidation lifecycle, and pre-Core failure states.
Negative/race tests and rollout gates did not cover missing, stale, mismatched, bypassed, interrupted, or unclassifiable evidence.

reviewReadiness.reason: All required verification passed and no residual risk remains.
expectedReviewFocus: Confirm the normative evidence object and ordered result rules represent passed, failed, incomplete, and not_run states without optimistic fallbacks.
Confirm GitHub and GitLab conclusions are conditional on candidate-bound terminal platform consumption and no-stale-bypass policy evidence.
Confirm ready_for_review remains independent of mergeSafety and GitLab Free remains head-only diagnostics.

userCorrectionsCaptured: Treat this as a critical upgrade and review it carefully until no flaws remain.
GitLab Free should not receive detailed compatibility coverage and must be stated as non-corresponding.
userCorrectionSolidification: Three iterative independent review cycles covered factual accuracy, completeness, consistency, clarity, and implementer usability; final accuracy, completeness, and consistency passes reported no findings.
The design derives mergeSafety from explicit construction, freshness, policy, Core, and evidence states rather than a platform or candidate-mode label.
GitLab Free remains concise head-only diagnostics and no synthetic compatibility design was reintroduced.

checkpointEvidence.detail: Reviewed the critical findings, official platform sources, fail-closed acceptance criteria, and user confirmation before modifying the design.

Reviewed the normative evidence state machine, authoritative adapter mappings, policy conditions, failure lifecycle, test/rollout coverage, and zero-finding final review results before repository verification.

knownGaps: No runtime adapter, CI integration, installer profile, or JSON Schema implementation is delivered by this documentation Work Item.

GitLab's documented merge-train enforcement is not production-ready at the time of this design, so the design conditionally withholds a merge-safe conclusion unless no-stale-bypass policy can be evidenced.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: A target-integrated candidate lacks terminal freshness proof.
evidence: Native Platform Adapters matrix and Evidence Strength conclusion matrix

scenarioCoverage.scenario: A GitLab Merge Train is bypassed or skip_merge_train avoids pipeline restart.
evidence: GitLab Premium and Ultimate adapter policy and Failure Semantics sections

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Candidate, base, source, event, or policy evidence is missing, stale, mismatched, delayed, interrupted, or unclassifiable.
evidence: Normative v1 evidence object, ordered result rules, Failure Semantics, and adapter test strategy

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Runtime and CI implementation remain outside this documentation Work Item.
evidence: Status, Non-Goals, Contract scope, and Contract outOfScope

intentAlignment.rationaleValidated: The design now prevents platform labels or target-integrated construction from becoming merge-safe claims without separately validated freshness, policy, Core, and evidence results.

overclaimPrevention: Do not claim runtime implementation or merge safety from platform availability alone; only the complete normative evidence combination may set mergeSafety to established.
